description.content:
The Purview API is used by the Microsoft Purview portal (purview.microsoft.com) for managing compliance, governance, and data security, including:

  - Data Infrastructure (entity discovery and search)
  - eDiscovery case management
  - Compliance Manager assessments
  - Audit log search
  - Governance Web Services (authorization, DLM, classification)
  - Data Governance Services
  - Security Copilot integration
  - Microsoft Graph proxy
  - Platform Services (shell, IRIS, Azure Purview)
  - Billing and licensing
  - DLP device management
  - Insider Risk Management
  - SharePoint Online settings

These APIs are accessed through the portal's API proxy layer at `purview.microsoft.com/apiproxy` and support delegated authentication flows.
They are used internally by the Purview portal and are not officially documented by Microsoft.

The proxy layer routes requests to various backend services using path prefixes:

| Prefix | Service |
| --- | --- |
| `/di/` | Data Infrastructure (entity discovery/search) — shared with Defender XDR |
| `/aedmcc/` | Advanced eDiscovery & Content Search |
| `/cpm/` | Compliance Manager |
| `/adtsch/` | Audit Search |
| `/gws/` | Governance Web Services (authorization/DLM) — shared with Defender XDR |
| `/dgs/` | Data Governance Services |
| `/medeina/` | Security Copilot (Medeina) — shared with Defender XDR |
| `/cdssecuritycopilot/` | CDS Security Copilot (legacy, see /medeina/) |
| `/msgraph/` | Microsoft Graph Proxy — shared with Defender XDR |
| `/purviewplatform/` | Purview Platform |
| `/cbill/` | Purview Billing |
| `/mtp/` | Microsoft Threat Protection (DLP devices) — shared with Defender XDR |
| `/insiderrisk/` | Insider Risk Management |
| `/spo/` | SharePoint Online |
| `/shell/` | Portal Shell — shared with Defender XDR |
| `/iris/` | IRIS Feature Selection |
| `/azurepurview/` | Azure Purview |
| `/ocps/` | Office Configuration |
| `/Ingestion/` | Compliance Ingestion |

**Cross-portal shared services:** Several backend services are shared with the Microsoft Defender XDR portal (security.microsoft.com).
The `/di/`, `/gws/`, `/medeina/`, `/msgraph/`, `/mtp/`, and `/shell/` prefixes route to the same backend services in both portals.
Purview is the primary consumer of `/di/` (Data Infrastructure) with 21+ endpoints for entity discovery and search.
The `/gws/` (Governance Web Services) prefix provides shared authorization and permission validation across both portals.

Documentation and specifications have been provided on a best efforts basis and may not be 100% accurate.
